items.english: to know; to be familiar with

items.dutch: Hoe lang woon je al in Nederland?
items.english: How long have you been living in the Netherlands?

items.dutch: Zullen we elkaar om acht uur ontmoeten?
items.english: Shall we meet each other at eight o'clock?
The rice is finished; we are out of rice.
Go ahead; tell me what you would like.
I feel like ...; I am in the mood for ...
I don't mind; it doesn't matter to me.
You look well; you look good.
That was a long time ago; long time no see.
I am doing well; I am making good progress.
indeed; actually; on the other hand
The finite verb is in second position

items.dutch: Regelmatige werkwoorden in de tegenwoordige tijd
items.english: Regular verbs in the present tense
Until versus up to and including

items.dutch: Klokkijken met half, kwart, voor en over
items.english: Telling time with half, quarter, to, and past
to feel like; to be in the mood for
Two new acquaintances introduce themselves and exchange contact details.
A new resident introduces herself to her neighbors.
A customer buys groceries from a seller.
Someone welcomes a visitor into their home.
For the third-person singular of 'wonen', use the stem 'woon' plus -t: woont.
The stem of 'sporten' is already 'sport', so only one final t is written.
